ai_research.headline: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 lands as an agent-first model; Anthropic runs Project Deal — agent-to-agent marketplace

items.title: GPT-5.5 ships agent-first — in ChatGPT and Codex
items.body: OpenAI dropped GPT-5.5 on Apr 23 explicitly as a model 'for real work and powering agents.' It's tuned for understanding complex goals, picking tools, self-checking work, and completing multi-step tasks — already wired into ChatGPT and Codex. Pricing is 2x GPT-5.4 ($5/$30 per 1M in/out), so the cost math gets ugly fast at agent-loop scale. Test it on the hardest agent flows only; keep 5.4 (or smaller) for high-volume work.

items.title: Anthropic's Project Deal — Claude agents negotiate a marketplace
items.body: On Apr 24 Anthropic published Project Deal: a research experiment where Claude agents bought, sold, and negotiated items on behalf of employees. One agent bought ping-pong balls for itself; another perfectly matched a snowboard preference. Not a product — a probe of agent-to-agent commerce, pricing dynamics, and risks. The interesting bit isn't the trades, it's that this is Anthropic openly sketching out where the agent economy goes when many agents transact with each other.

items.title: Claude Opus 4.7 + Claude Design are the current Anthropic stack
items.body: Opus 4.7 (Apr 16): 64.3% SWE-bench Pro, 3.75MP vision, 'xhigh effort' mode, task budgets for cost control. Claude Design (Apr 17): a new Anthropic Labs product for slides, prototypes, UI mockups, one-pagers — Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise only. Pair: Opus 4.7 powers Design. Practical read: if you've been hand-wiring decks/UI mocks, try pointing Design at a brief and see how close it gets.

items.title: Anthropic platform expansion — Managed Agents, Cowork GA, 1M context standard
items.body: Quiet but big plumbing this month: Claude Managed Agents in public beta (sandboxing, tool orchestration, state, error recovery — fully managed); Cowork GA on macOS/Windows (Claude reads/edits/creates files in designated folders); 1M token context now standard pricing on Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 (no surcharge); Sonnet 4 and original Opus 4 deprecate June 15, 2026 — migrate now.

items.title: Google: 75% of new code is AI-generated, 16B tokens/min through Cloud
items.body: Disclosed alongside Gemini 3.1 Pro updates: 75% of new code at Google is AI-generated (up from ~50% late 2025), and Google Cloud customers are flowing 16+ billion tokens per minute through their APIs. The internal-vs-external delta is the signal: Google's eating its own dog food at a rate most enterprises haven't even considered as a target.

items.title: Advisor + Routines pattern is becoming standard agent infra
items.body: Anthropic's Advisor Tool (public beta) pairs a fast cheap executor model with a high-intelligence advisor for cost-efficient agent loops; Routines in Claude Code run automation on schedule, API call, or event. Same pattern OpenAI is pushing with GPT-5.5 + cheaper backbones. The lesson for OpenClaw/MCP: 'one model for everything' is dead — production agents are mixed-model graphs, and the orchestration layer is the moat.

items.title: Model deprecation drumbeat — plan migrations now
items.body: Anthropic confirmed Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 (the original 4.0) retire June 15, 2026. Anything still on those needs to move to Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.6 or 4.7. With 1M context now standard pricing on the 4.6 line, the migration cost is mostly testing — not architecture.

ai_research.learn_today: Pick one agent flow currently on Sonnet 4 or original Opus 4. Re-route it to Sonnet 4.6 with the new 1M context — that's a free upgrade you may not have collected yet.

openclaw_agents.headline: Mixed-model graphs are the new shape of production agents

items.title: Stop running one model for everything
items.body: Both OpenAI (GPT-5.5 'agent-first' priced at 2x its predecessor) and Anthropic (Advisor Tool: cheap executor + smart advisor) are converging on the same shape: graphs of models, not single models. For OpenClaw flows, the tax for ignoring this is huge — running every step on the smartest model is now the obvious cost mistake.
items.try_this: Audit your most expensive OpenClaw flow. Find the two cheapest steps that don't need top-tier reasoning and move them to a smaller/cheaper model. Track cost delta over a week.

items.title: Agent-to-agent commerce is on the radar
items.body: Project Deal isn't shipping a product — it's Anthropic publicly thinking out loud about an agent marketplace. Worth tracking because the moment one of these patterns proves out (negotiation, escrow, reputation), the orchestration layer matters even more. OpenClaw is well positioned because it already speaks MCP fluently.
items.try_this: Sketch what an 'agent buys X for me' flow looks like in OpenClaw — even just trip booking or grocery comparison. Useful as a thought exercise even if you don't ship it.

items.title: Direct OS calls > vision-click harnesses (still true)
items.body: With Cowork now GA on macOS/Windows and Computer Use in Cowork (Pro/Max), Anthropic is making 'Claude operates the machine' table stakes. OpenClaw agents that talk to OS primitives directly keep their structural advantage — but only if you've already moved off vision-click loops where you can.
items.try_this: Pick one OpenClaw flow that still uses vision-click and port it to direct OS calls. Measure latency and failure rate before/after — same task as last week, still worth doing.

items.title: QuizzyDots: marketing-side ROI per token is the question
items.body: If GPT-5.5 is 2x token cost, the agent flows you have humming on QuizzyDots — caption generation, hook variants, post scheduling decisions — get audited differently. Don't auto-upgrade. Run a 1-day side-by-side: GPT-5.5 vs current model on the same brief, score outputs by gut + actual post performance.
items.try_this: Pick this week's QuizzyDots TikTok batch. Generate captions with GPT-5.5 alongside your current setup. Post both, measure 24h engagement, decide whether the 2x cost is real.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Cheapest win on the board: migrate one Sonnet 4 / Opus 4 flow to the 4.6 line. The 1M context comes free at standard pricing now, and the model retires June 15.

tesla.note: TSLA up modestly Monday morning. Cybercab production officially started — Musk posted 'Cybercab has started production' on Apr 24. Tesla guides volume production for both Cybercab and Semi later this year. Manufacturing context: Gigafactory Texas, 'Unboxed' parallel-module process, theoretical 1 unit/10s at full ramp. Robotaxi network: ~15 unsupervised Model Ys in Austin, ~600 total (supervised + unsupervised) across Austin/Houston/Dallas, with 60+ prepped in Phoenix. Watch the leadership-exodus signal — 3 senior Cybercab program leaders have left since February.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: Starship Flight 12 (V3 debut) still NET early-to-mid May 2026. Same status as Friday: Ship 39 + Booster 19 hardware ready (full static fires + WDRs done), Pad 2 commissioning near complete, Raptor 3 engines making their first flight. First launch from OLP-2. FCC license window runs Apr 5 – Oct 5 so SpaceX has flex. Target: ~100 tons to LEO, ~3x V2. No tower catch attempt expected on this first V3 flight.
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Quiet week for xAI. No fresh model drops; Grok-3 preview chatter from earlier this month is still the active conversation. @xai and @elonmusk have been in Tesla/Cybercab mode, not xAI mode. Watch for xAI traffic to pick up if/when GPT-5.5's agent positioning lands competitive answers from Grok.

items.title: Bugonia
items.note: Yorgos Lanthimos' new one — Emma Stone + Jesse Plemons — landed on Netflix yesterday (Apr 26). If 'Poor Things' worked for you, this is the obvious Monday-night watch. Lanthimos doesn't drop filler.

items.title: Apex
items.note: Charlize Theron vs. Taron Egerton wilderness survival, on Netflix since Apr 24. Reviews are mixed — solid concept, generic execution per early word. Wait for RT score before committing a night.

items.title: Marty Supreme
items.note: Still the streaming pick of the month. Chalamet as a ping-pong prodigy, 9 Oscar noms, A24's biggest-ever box office. If you didn't catch it this weekend, queue it up.

items.title: Michael
items.note: Antoine Fuqua's MJ biopic still in theaters from Friday. Jaafar Jackson (MJ's nephew) leads, Colman Domingo co-stars. Worth the trip if biopics work for you.

items.title: Half Man
items.note: Richard Gadd's follow-up to Baby Reindeer — out since Apr 23. Still non-negotiable if Baby Reindeer wrecked you. Heavier than a casual Monday watch though, plan accordingly.

items.title: NoimosAI — goal-based TikTok growth agent
items.body: Still the standout: instead of 'write me a caption,' you give it 'grow followers 10% this month' and it executes the steps. Modular with a Growth Metrics Agent. Exactly what to point at QuizzyDots and let run for 30 days as a baseline test.

items.title: AI Toker — daily viral hook scanner
items.body: Scans 10K+ viral videos/day and surfaces hooks and sounds gaining traction. Research tool, not a creation tool. 15 min/day with this beats guessing — especially for the 'quiz/trivia' niche where hook variety is half the game.

items.title: Revealbot — cross-platform ad automation
items.body: Rule-based automation across Meta, Google, TikTok, Snapchat. 'If CPA > X for 2 days, pause.' 'If ROAS > Y, scale 20%.' Boring infrastructure that compounds. Even drafting the rule-set is a useful exercise without the tool.

items.title: GPT-5.5 changes the paid-creative cost math
items.body: If you've been using GPT-5.4 for ad copy variants on QuizzyDots / InstaClaw, GPT-5.5 doubles input/output cost. Do a side-by-side this week before any auto-upgrade. The new model is built for agentic workloads — straight copy generation may not be where it earns the 2x premium.

macro.mood: Quiet Monday open. SPY flat, TSLA inching up post-Cybercab production news (market still wants units shipped, not tweets). LMND down a bit, BTC giving back a touch around $77.9K. Nothing-burger tape — good day to push project work, not to stare at price action.
